intro: Chaseopsis wercklei, also known as Werckheim's chaseopsis, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, native to Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits, such as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Chaseopsis wercklei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties.
